description: Apply Standard II (Integrity of Capital Markets) — Material Nonpublic Information and Market Manipulation — to investment research, trading, and execution practices

ii_a_mnpi.rule: Do not act or cause others to act on material nonpublic information
ii_a_mnpi.material_test: Reasonable investor would consider it important
ii_a_mnpi.nonpublic_test: Not widely disseminated or selectively disclosed
ii_a_mnpi.mosaic_theory: Analysis combining public + nonmaterial nonpublic is allowed
ii_a_mnpi.firewall_procedures: Physical and procedural separation between departments

ii_b_market_manipulation.transaction_based: Wash trades, cornering, pump and dump
ii_b_market_manipulation.intent: Purpose is to mislead market participants

recommended_procedures.restricted_list: Prevent trading in securities with MNPI

investigative_implications.disclosure: Encourage issuer to disclose the MNPI
investigative_implications.abstain: If not disclosed, abstain from trading and from recommending

errors.message: substandard must be ii_a or ii_b

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume selectively-disclosed information is public
anti_patterns.why: Issuer disclosure to one analyst does not cure nonpublic status; trading on it breaches II(A)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not rely on mosaic theory to bypass information barriers
anti_patterns.why: Mosaic combines nonmaterial public with nonmaterial nonpublic; one material nonpublic item taints the analysis

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat market-making or liquidity provision as exempt from II(B)
anti_patterns.why: Transactions designed to mislead remain manipulation even if carried out by market makers; intent is the test
